prevalence.source: European primary care and pediatric infection epidemiology syntheses

burden.dalyPerCapita: low; high healthcare utilization and school absenteeism

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical assessment; rapid antigen testing/culture used in many settings to guide antibiotics; evaluate for peritonsillar abscess and other complications

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Millions of episodes annually across the EU
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High; antibiotic stewardship supported by rapid testing in many systems

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Rapid strep testing widely available in many primary care settings; culture when needed
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care for viral; antibiotics for confirmed streptococcal infection; ENT referral for recurrent severe disease and tonsillectomy evaluation
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Balancing antibiotic stewardship with patient expectations; variable primary care access across countries
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: High availability of diagnostics and effective antibiotics; structured referral pathways
